paper_title: Taxonomy and abundance behaviour of five major groups of Miocene discoasters at IODP Site U1338

methods.description: Analysis of sediment composition and depth scale adjustments for Site U1338 to understand the sedimentation rates and age estimates.

steps.description: The composition of Site U1338 sediments changes from chalks in the upper lower Miocene sequence to oozes in the upper Miocene, with frequent and rapid changes in the proportions between calcareous and biosiliceous components.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze sediment composition changes over time

steps.description: The interval examined represents a continuous 336 m thick, spliced sequence from three holes of upper lower through uppermost Miocene sediment between 406 and 72 m CCCSF-A.
steps.searchable_summary: Process sediment sequence data for depth scale

steps.description: This revised splice of Site U1338 is used here, and all depths given below and in plots refer to the revised CCSF-A depth scale of Wilkens et al. (2013), divided by 1.107.
steps.searchable_summary: Adjust depth scale using revised CCSF-A

methods.description: Preparation and analysis of smear-slides to determine the abundance of Discoaster species using light microscopy.

steps.description: The 494 smear-slides were prepared following the technique of Bown & Young (1998).
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare smear-slides using Bown & Young technique

steps.description: Smear-slides were analysed using a light microscope at 1250× magnification.

Abundance data were obtained by counting the relative abundance of Discoaster species relative to the total number of discoasters, expressed as a percentage, and the number of specimens in a prefixed area on the smear-slide (number of specimens per mm²).
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate relative abundance of Discoaster species

methods.description: Morphometric analyses of Discoaster species using image analysis software to study their evolution and biostratigraphy.

steps.description: Morphometric analyses were performed using the image analysis software Image-Pro Plus 6.2.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform morphometric analysis with Image-Pro Plus
